name: Beta-hydroxybutyrate extended C. elegans lifespan through HDAC-linked biology
summary: Beta-hydroxybutyrate supports histone deacetylase modulation in animal lifespan, with broad metabolic pathway overlap.
statement: D-beta-hydroxybutyrate, described as a histone deacetylase inhibitor, extended C. elegans mean lifespan and required DAF-16/FOXO, SKN-1/Nrf, SIR-2.1, and AMPK-linked pathways.
population_or_model: Caenorhabditis elegans lifespan and pathway-dependence assays
quantitative_note: The study reported approximately 20% mean lifespan extension.
caveats: This is worm evidence, not mammalian or human aging evidence.
Beta-hydroxybutyrate is a metabolic signal with multiple non-HDAC mechanisms.
The result should not be read as clinical evidence for ketogenic interventions.
